sc_sutta: 30 “Mendicants, I see two reasons to frequent remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest. What two? Seeing happiness for oneself in this life, and having sympathy for future generations. I see two reasons to frequent remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest.”
sutta: The buddha said monks it is because i observe these two results therein that i am given to dwelling in lonely spots in solitary lodging in the forest what to observing my own pleasant way of living in this very life and feeling compassion for future generations these are the two results that's the end of the sutta
commentary: Here the buddha is saying that he likes to live in secluded places live alone in the forests or caves etc for two reasons one is um his own uh pleasant way of living that means he has a pleasant abiding for himself because when when a monk stays alone there is nobody to disturb him and he is very free to do what he likes and especially if a monk is good in meditation he can just spend his time in meditation which gives him a lot of bliss and a lot of advanced advantage in the spiritual life because the buddha said that the cultivation of the jhanas will uh give you four advantages uh cultivation of the mind and cultivation of samadhi and concentration give you four advantages namely the four and he also said somewhere i think in the sutra or in the vinaya he says when he lives alone in the forest then he can he feels so free and that even when he wants to answer the calls of nature he doesn't have to look around to see whether anybody is around and the second reason is feeling compassion for future generations this is because of compassion for future generations he is showing the way for monks in other words encouraging monks to stay in secluded places and to cultivate themselves and he also always encourage monks to practice alone stay in secluded places and practice alone but of course not all monks can do it unless you come to that stage where you can live alone if you go and try to live alone when you are not ready it can have a negative effect instead of a positive the other thing i would like to mention here concerning this utah is in the mahayana teachings there is a little bit of contradiction also concerning this one because in the mahayana buddhist precepts one of the minor bodhisattva precepts is that a buddhist is not it's not allowed or forbidden to stay alone in the uh quiet place around near a secluded place he's not allowed according to the bodhisattva precept to stay alone whereas here even the buddha is already enlightened and yet he likes to stay alone and he also encourages his disciples who are not enlightened yet two also dwell alone to cultivate themselves the other person who is famous for staying alone is mahakasapa the very ascetic monk disciple of the buddha now
